name: Scale Ruler Intent
summary: The foundational scale primitive — map a scalar to a length or a position on one shared, labeled axis in real units, and aggregate a stack of scalars (stacked card heights → a lane ETA). The layer that sits BELOW charts, layout, and animation, exactly as d3-scale is a standalone package because scales are reused everywhere; Web Charts (we:src/_data/projects/webcharts.json, #105) composes its marks on top. Adopts native-first: `<progress>` renders a scalar as a length, CSS `aspect-ratio` maps a ratio to a box. Reference marks (a datum/threshold line = a guide on the ruler, Fork 4 folded in) are part of the contract; the region-mask visual treatment (desaturating the crossed side) is FUI presentation, NOT the WE contract. Grounded in d3-scale, design-tool rulers+guides (Photoshop/Illustrator/Figma/Sketch/CAD), and Vega-Lite positional/size encodings. Minted from #2533 Fork 1 (+Fork 4).

type.description: The mapping family — the d3-scale vocabulary adopted verbatim. `linear` (default) maps a continuous domain to a continuous range proportionally; `log` maps a log-scaled domain (orders of magnitude); `time` maps a temporal domain (the board's minute-ruler); `band` maps an ordinal/categorical domain to evenly-divided positions. The family is the scale's identity, never a presentation choice.

orientation.description: Which way the labeled axis runs. `horizontal` (default) is the native `<progress>` shape — a scalar rendered left-to-right as a length. `vertical` runs bottom-to-top (the board's time ruler). Orientation is the axis's direction, decoupled from the mapping family above.

aggregate.description: How a stack of scalars on the same axis reduces to one readout. `none` (default) is a single scalar → a single length/position. `sum` stacks scalars and reports the total (stacked card heights → a lane ETA — the board case). `extent` reports the min–max span the stack occupies. Aggregation is a property of the shared axis, so every stacked value reads in the same units.

reference.description: Reference marks on the scale (Fork 4 folded in — a guide belongs to the ruler, as in Photoshop/Figma). `none` (default) draws a bare axis. `tick` marks a single reference value — a datum/threshold/`today` line, exactly what d3-axis reference rules and Vega's `rule` mark draw. `region` marks a span between two values (a crossed-threshold band). WE owns the SEMANTIC marking of the value/span; the visual region-MASK treatment (e.g. `backdrop-filter: grayscale` on the crossed side) is FUI presentation, outside this contract.

description:
What This Is

The foundational scale primitive — the map from a scalar value to a length or a position on one shared, labeled axis, in real units. It is the layer that sits below charts, layout, and animation: d3-scale is a standalone package precisely because a scale is reused everywhere — the same domain→range mapping drives a bar's length, a mark's position, an animation's easing, and a color ramp. Minted from #2533 Fork 1 (with Fork 4 folded in).

The core map

A binding names the shared axis and the value: { scalar, unit, pxPer, cap?, axisRef }. pxPer is the scalar→length factor (pixels per one domain unit — the board's 1pt ≈ 9min ≈ 17px); unit keeps the axis labeled in real units; cap optionally clamps the mapped length/position; axisRef ties every value that shares one ruler to the same domain→range mapping, so a lane's stacked heights read in one coordinate system. The map produces a length (a magnitude, the native <progress> case) or a position (a coordinate on the axis).

Aggregate — a stack reads as a total

Because every value on a shared axis is in the same units, a stack of scalars reduces to one readout: sum stacks card heights into a lane ETA, extent reports the span the stack occupies. This is the residue once Web Charts' marks (Vega-Lite positional/size encodings) are subtracted — the bare scale is exactly what Charts composes above.

Reference marks — a guide on the ruler (Fork 4)

A datum/threshold line is a reference mark on the scale, not a separate primitive — a guide belongs to the ruler exactly as a Photoshop/Figma guide belongs to its ruler, never to a separate tool. tick marks one reference value (a today line, a target, a WIP-limit — what d3-axis reference rules and Vega's rule mark draw); region marks a span between two values (a crossed-threshold band). WE owns the semantic marking (this value/this span is marked); the visual region-mask treatment — desaturating the crossed side with backdrop-filter — is FUI presentation, outside this contract.

Native-first baseline

The thin native primitives this leans on: <progress> renders a scalar as a length (a linear/horizontal scale with no marks), and CSS aspect-ratio maps a ratio to a box. Extend from those; do not reinvent below the platform floor.

Distinct from Web Charts

"It's Web Charts minus the marks" argues for extracting the scale as the foundational layer, not against a mint: Web Charts (#105) composes this scale and adds marks (bars, points, lines) on top. The scale is the reused substrate; a chart is one consumer of it, alongside layout and animation. Prior art the recurring-pattern grounding rests on: d3-scale as a standalone foundational package, and design-tool rulers + guides (Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, Sketch, CAD — a persistent labeled axis mapping document position to screen position in units, with guides as snapped reference marks).

Composition

Composes Web Theme (axis/tick/guide colors resolve from tokens, never author hex). Web Charts composes this scale as its positional substrate. WE owns this intent contract; FUI ships the rendered ruler block and the region-mask presentation.

Interface Protocol

type ScaleType = 'linear' | 'log' | 'time' | 'band';type ScaleOrientation = 'horizontal' | 'vertical';type ScaleAggregate = 'none' | 'sum' | 'extent';type ScaleReference = 'none' | 'tick' | 'region';interface ScaleBinding {    // the scalar domain value to map    scalar: number;    // real-world unit of the domain (e.g. 'min', 'px', 'MB')    unit: string;    // pixels per one domain unit — the scalar→length factor    pxPer: number;    // optional clamp on the mapped length/position    cap?: number;    // shared-axis key: values sharing an axisRef share one domain→range mapping    axisRef: string;}interface ScaleRulerIntent {    // the mapping family (d3-scale)    type: ScaleType;    // which way the labeled axis runs    orientation: ScaleOrientation;    // how a stack of scalars on the shared axis reduces to one readout    aggregate: ScaleAggregate;    // reference marks on the axis (a datum tick or a crossed-threshold region)    reference: ScaleReference;    // scalar → length | position on the axis, in real units    map(binding: ScaleBinding): number;}
